mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance improves.
failure_condition: Correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.15, p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that portends earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The interaction effect is stronger than either signal alone because both reflect consumer confidence collapse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% year-over-year signals expanding productive capacity and pricing power that supports corporate margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO growth > 1.5% are not significantly higher than periods of slower growth (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (Pearson r > -0.12, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.5%) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 3.5%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS > 3.5% simultaneously
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at levels above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than quarters with slower growth (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,200 thousand units annualized signals weakening residential investment and forward consumer wealth effects that reduce aggregate demand and equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units and remains below that level for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: housing market contraction or demand shock
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances where HOUST is below 1,200 thousand units, or correlation between HOUST levels and forward returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.10, p > 0.05).
